title: I Made ChatGPT and TradingView Have a Baby
author: Asoley_rois
selftext:
Background: I'm a CS student who recently got into trading.

I saw TradingView and thought to add AI to it.

Over the weekend I made a little tool that looks like TradingView but also allows you to prompt your chart. So now you can ask it to add indicators, create code to make strategies, and run some backtesting.

https://reddit.com/link/1ruxlak/video/tr9gxmuigbpg1/player

comments.author: TumbleweedOk1206
comments.body: so it just generates strategies. can i directly use them to trade?

replies.author: Asoley_rois
replies.body:
i don't think it'll be easy to directly connect to brokers, but you can definitely use the code that it makes to run the same strategy

need to figure out how to make it easy to deploy the strategy though

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
This is actually a really cool idea, especially for newer traders who struggle with Pine Script or don’t fully understand indicator settings yet.

A couple thoughts/questions that might help you iterate:

- How are you handling hallucinations? If someone prompts a strategy like “optimize for max Sharpe on BTC 1h,” are you constraining the AI so it doesn’t overfit or generate unrealistic assumptions?
- Does it generate clean, editable Pine Script, or is it abstracted behind your UI? Being able to inspect and tweak the raw code would be huge for learning.
- How are you validating backtests? Slippage, fees, and lookahead bias are where a lot of AI-generated strategies fall apart.

From a UX standpoint, the real value IMO isn’t just “add RSI for me,” but:
- Explaining *why* a certain indicator fits the user’s idea  
- Suggesting parameter ranges  
- Warning about common pitfalls (e.g., over-optimization)

If you’re open to feedback, a killer feature would be:  
“Explain why this strategy lost money in this period.”  
That kind of contextual analysis would differentiate it from just being a Pine code generator.

Overall, as a CS student project, this is genuinely impressive. If you keep tightening the guardrails around bad strategy generation, this could become a really solid trader workflow tool.

replies.author: Asoley_rois
replies.body: thank you! it's now public on [heyastral.ai](http://heyastral.ai)
